_meta.notes: All values are approximate. Manually updated. Not financial advice.

global_macro_risk.label: Global Macro Risk
global_macro_risk.notes: 1=low risk, 5=high risk. Current drivers: US tariff escalation and trade fragmentation, China structural slowdown, elevated geopolitical tensions (Ukraine, Middle East), high global public debt levels.

Perth.rationale: Mining economy resilience, very low vacancy, strong interstate and overseas migration inflows

Brisbane.rationale: Population surge from interstate migration, SEQ infrastructure pipeline, relative affordability vs Sydney/Melbourne

Adelaide.rationale: Persistently low vacancy, steady interstate migration, smaller and tighter market

Sydney.rationale: Tight supply supports prices but affordability ceiling limits further upside; balanced risk/reward

Canberra.rationale: Public sector employment stability but lower population growth and capital growth potential

Hobart.rationale: Cooling from pandemic-era peak, tourism-dependent economy, lower liquidity and smaller investor base

Melbourne.rationale: Oversupply risk in apartment market, elevated land tax burden on investors, weaker investor sentiment

Darwin.rationale: High price volatility, low market liquidity, weak underlying demand fundamentals

recent_shifts.text: The RBA raised rates in February and March 2026. Policy remains restrictive, and markets currently price in the possibility of further hikes — though the path ahead is uncertain.
Inflation has eased from its 2022–23 peak but remains slightly above the RBA's 2–3% target band.
Unemployment has drifted up from the 3.4% trough. Forward indicators — job ads, hours worked — suggest further softening is possible in 2026.
Wage growth has outpaced inflation over the past year, improving real purchasing power for buyers.
Migration remains well above the long-run average, though the pace has moderated from the 2022–23 post-COVID surge above 500k. Still a meaningful demand driver.
Dwelling approvals remain well below what is needed to meet population growth — supply constraints persist.
National vacancy rates remain near record lows, keeping rental yields elevated and investor returns firm.
Sentiment improved modestly after the first rate cut, but remains below the long-run neutral level of 100.
Risk is elevated. US tariff escalation, a slowing China, geopolitical tensions, and high global debt levels create a genuinely uncertain external backdrop for Australia.
